Math & Science

Engineering & Construction · Termotecnica & HVAC

BTU to kWh Converter

Convert BTU ↔ kWh with sig figs, Joules/Wh outputs, and an explicit reminder about energy vs power (BTU/h, kW) so you size HVAC and electrical loads correctly.

Scientific Energy HVAC
0
Pro outputs

    Quick mode for fast conversions; switch to Pro to see Joules, Wh, and BTU context.

    When to use it

    • HVAC sizing: cooling/heating capacity labeled in BTU/h; electrical billing in kWh.
    • Appliance comparison: gas vs electric heaters, heat pumps, boilers.
    • Energy storage: battery discharge (kWh) vs thermal storage (BTU).

    Inputs & outputs

    • Energy value in BTU or kWh (sig figs supported).
    • Direction toggle (BTU → kWh or kWh → BTU).
    • Pro mode: Joules, Wh, and original unit echo for QA.

    Theory & formulas

    • Energy link: 1 kWh ≈ 3412 BTU; inverse 1 BTU ≈ 0.00029307107 kWh.
    • Definitions: BTU heats 1 lb of water by 1°F; kWh is 1 kW sustained for an hour.
    • Energy vs power: BTU/kWh are energy; BTU/h and kW are power (rate). Don’t mix them when sizing equipment.

    Examples

    • 12,000 BTU/h AC ≈ 3.52 kW cooling capacity (steady hourly energy).
    • 5 kWh from a home battery ≈ 17,060 BTU of stored energy.
    • 40,000 BTU furnace cycle ≈ 11.73 kWh; check electrical breaker sizing vs gas input.

    FAQ

    BTU or BTU/h?

    BTU is energy; BTU/h is power. This converter handles energy—size equipment with BTU/h or kW separately.

    Where does 3412 come from?

    It connects the joule definition to the mechanical equivalent of heat linking watt-hours and BTU.

    Can I use sig figs?

    Yes—use the sig fig selector to keep precision realistic for specs and billing.
